macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: US LEI Falls Further in January Amid Consumer Weakness and Building Permits Decline
items.summary: The Conference Board Leading Economic Index (LEI) declined in January 2026, driven by retreating consumer expectations and softer building permits, signaling headwinds to economic activity. GDP growth forecast revised down to 2.0% y/y for 2026, not yet reflecting Iran war impacts.

items.title: Trump Criticizes NATO Over Ineffective Response to Iran Strait of Hormuz Crisis
items.summary: US President Trump blasted NATO allies for failing to support during heightened tensions in the Strait of Hormuz amid the Iran war, stating they only reached out after stabilization and that the US does not need their help.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Liquidations Reach $817M Amid US-Iran Tensions
items.summary: Crypto markets experienced $817 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, with $661 million from short positions, driven by risk-off sentiment from US-Iran conflict. Despite volatility, Bitcoin dipped but markets showed resilience amid geopolitical developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Surges to Two-Month High of $78,000 on Easing Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Bitcoin hit a two-month high near $78,000, up over 5% following Iran's announcement reopening the Strait of Hormuz and easing US-Iran tensions. Ethereum climbed to around $2,440, with total market cap surging 3.51% to $2.62 trillion.

items.title: Kraken Parent Payward Acquires Derivatives Exchange Bitnomial for Up to $550M
items.summary: Kraken's parent company Payward agreed to buy Cheyenne-based derivatives exchange Bitnomial for up to $550 million in a cash-and-stock deal valuing Payward at $20 billion. The acquisition provides Kraken with advanced crypto settlement and 24/7 trading infrastructure.

items.title: Crypto Futures Liquidations Hit $565M, Mostly Short Positions
items.summary: Global crypto futures saw $565 million in liquidations in 24 hours, with $380 million in Bitcoin and $165 million in Ethereum positions, over 87% being shorts amid a sharp price surge. This highlights high leverage and volatility in current trading.

items.title: Crypto Market Cap Surges 3.51% to $2.62 Trillion; DeFi and NFT Activity Rises
items.summary: Total crypto market capitalization rose 3.51% to $2.62 trillion, with Bitcoin up 3.26% to $77,099 and Ethereum up 3.55% to $2,409. DeFi TVL increased 1.66% to $99.9 billion, and NFT sales volume grew 2.25%.

items.title: Crypto Stocks Rally as Bitcoin Breaks Two-Month High After Iran Strait Reopening
items.summary: Crypto-related stocks like Strategy, Robinhood, and Coinbase rose following Bitcoin's surge to a two-month high after Iran reopened the Strait of Hormuz. President Trump commented on Truth Social about the Strait being 'open for business.'

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos AI Model Sparks Alarm in Trump Administration Over Cybersecurity Capabilities
items.summary: Anthropic has developed Mythos, a powerful AI model excelling at uncovering and exploiting cybersecurity vulnerabilities, leading to concerns in the Trump administration. The company decided not to release it publicly due to its advanced hacking abilities, demonstrated by 181 successful exploits on Opus 4.6.

items.title: Anthropic Releases Claude Opus 4.7, Potentially the Most Powerful Public AI Model
items.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Opus 4.7, an upgrade from Opus 4.6, hailed as the most powerful publicly available AI model to date. This release is part of a wave of major AI updates including Claude Design and other company announcements.

items.title: AI Agents Reshaping Corporate Structures and Erasing Traditional Job Ladders
items.summary: AI agents are becoming a digital workforce in companies, automating tasks and disrupting traditional corporate roles, according to Writer CEO May Habib. This trend signals rapid changes in enterprise AI adoption and the future of work.

items.title: Experts Warn of Rising AI-Generated Misinformation in Iran War
items.summary: Forensic experts highlight the increase in AI-generated videos spreading misinformation amid the war with Iran, produced by both sides. This underscores growing concerns over AI's role in geopolitical conflicts.

items.title: Economist Alex Imas Discusses AI's Unique Disruption to Labor Markets
items.summary: University of Chicago professor Alex Imas argues that AI's rapid development could disrupt labor markets differently from past technologies due to its generality and speed. He challenges the view that new jobs will naturally balance out the impacts.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: PRAGMATA Game Receives New Walkthrough Coverage
items.summary: A new walkthrough gameplay video for PRAGMATA Part 3 was released, highlighting its unique gameplay experience. The game has a listed release date of April 17, 2026.

items.title: Balatro Red Bull Boss Rush Tournament Features Top Streamers
items.summary: Streamers including Ludwig, FrostPrime, Feinberg, and others compete in a knockout tournament using a custom Red Bull mod for Balatro, focusing on speedrunning boss battles.

items.title: Joey's Retro Newscast Covers Latest Emulation and Handheld Updates
items.summary: Recent developments include MisterFPGA achievements, NetherSX2 Turnip updates, AYN Thor changes, MagicX delays, and hardware news on devices like Lenovo Legion Go 2 and OneXPlayer models.

items.title: Dungeon Crawler Carl RPG Crowdfunding Explodes with $5M Day One
items.summary: The Dungeon Crawler Carl role-playing game raised nearly $5 million on its first day, amid a surge in tabletop crowdfunding including other major campaigns.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Intel Launches Core Series 3 Processors for Affordable Laptops
items.summary: Intel has introduced the Core Series 3 processors targeting more affordable laptops, aiming to expand access to high-performance computing in the entry-level market.

items.title: Meta Raises Prices on Quest 3 and Quest 3S Headsets Due to Memory Crisis
items.summary: Meta is increasing prices for its Quest 3 and 3S VR headsets amid rising memory chip costs exacerbated by market shortages.

items.title: Samsung Galaxy Z TriFold Completely Sold Out After Limited Run
items.summary: Samsung announced that its Galaxy Z TriFold, a limited-edition tri-fold smartphone, is now fully sold out.

items.title: Quantum Motion Deploys First Full-Stack Silicon Quantum Computer
items.summary: Quantum Motion has achieved a milestone by deploying the first full-stack quantum computer using standard silicon chip fabrication processes, potentially accelerating scalable quantum systems.

items.title: Google and Qualcomm Push Android PCs into Mainstream
items.summary: Google and Qualcomm are advancing Android PCs to challenge Microsoft and Apple in personal computing by scaling the mobile OS to desktops.

items.title: Casely Recalls Over 400,000 Wireless Power Pods Over Fire Risks
items.summary: Casely is recalling more than 400,000 portable wireless power banks due to fire and burn hazards, as issued by the CPSC.

items.title: India Drops Plan to Force Apple to Preinstall State-Owned App on iPhones
items.summary: India has abandoned its plan requiring Apple to preinstall a state-owned app on iPhones, marking a policy shift.
